title: Local AI Agents Masterclass (Gemma 4 + OpenCode + Odysseus tutorial)
summary: Jordan Urbs demonstrates how to run two types of AI agents locally: Odysseus, a workspace agent for task automation and multi-agent collaboration, and OpenCode, a code generation agent. The tutorial covers installation via Docker, running Gemma 4 locally through Ollama, integrating Venice API as a fallback, and building applications using natural language commands. The core principle taught is to build with frontier models and execute with local ones.
writeup: The tutorial walks through running two local AI agents: Odysseus, a workspace agent for task automation and multi-agent collaboration, and OpenCode, a code generation agent. It covers installation via Docker, serving Gemma 4 locally through Ollama, wiring in the Venice API as a fallback, and building applications with natural language commands. The guiding principle is to build with frontier models and execute with local ones, which keeps inference on-device while reserving stronger models for harder steps.
